events.title: Afghanistan: Deadly flash floods hit Nuristan province
events.description: Some 20 people have been killed and more than 80 injured, with the city of Parun hit particularly hard. A lack of infrastructure and the effects of climate change have left the country vulnerable to natural disaster.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Afghanistan: Deadly flash floods hit Nuristan province

events.title: First Nations groups devastated by Canada’s wildfires say government did not help
events.description: Chiefs in Ontario say they were left to self-evacuate before homes burned to ground, after being told there was no imminent threat Fast-moving wildfires have burned two First Nations communities in Canada to the ground, as members said they were left to self-evacuate without government help or emergency alerts. Thirteen communities in Ontario province have been ordered to evacuate because of wildfires, most of them First Nations. The Ontario premier, Doug Ford, told reporters on Monday that close to 2,000 people had been evacuated so far to cities including Toronto and Niagara Falls. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ First Nations groups devastated by Canada’s wildfires say government did not help

events.title: ‘A big job ahead of him’: world leaders congratulate Andy Burnham as he becomes Britain’s PM
events.description: Beyond the diplomatic platitudes global media focuses on the ‘immense’ scale of the challenge he faces in the job UK politics live – latest updates World leaders have warmly congratulated Andy Burnham on his entry into 10 Downing Street as Britain’s seventh prime minister in a decade, but international media largely focused on the scale of the challenges awaiting him. Emmanuel Macron, France’s president, said he hoped for a deeper relationship “in the service of our peoples, and by giving new momentum to the partnership between the UK and the EU” rekindled by Burnham’s predecessor, Keir Starmer. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘A big job ahead of him’: world leaders congratulate Andy Burnham as he becomes Britain’s PM

events.title: Djibouti: Emergency alert spotlights sharp hunger spike
events.description: More than 256,000 people in Djibouti – around 25 per cent of the population – are likely to face crisis or emergency level hunger over the coming months, a UN-backed global food monitoring network warned in a new report released Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Djibouti: Emergency alert spotlights sharp hunger spike

events.title: Ukraine: Deadly Russian attacks severely damage aid warehouses
events.description: Russian attacks and drone strikes killed at least two children and injured dozens more across Ukraine over recent days while severely damaging three critical aid warehouses, UN agencies reported on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Deadly Russian attacks severely damage aid warehouses

events.title: Venezuela: IOM warns of potential El Niño threat to families displaced by earthquake
events.description: Thousands of people remain displaced three weeks after the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela and the possible formation of a strong El Niño climate pattern could worsen their already dire situation, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela: IOM warns of potential El Niño threat to families displaced by earthquake

events.title: India tunnel disaster at a Himalayan hydropower project leaves 10 dead and 17 missing
events.description: A suspected gas explosion has caused a tunnel collapse at a hydropower project in India's Sikkim state, near the Chinese border, leaving at least 11 people dead and 14 missing

events.title: Russia-Ukraine war: First half of 2026 sees sharp rise in civilian casualties
events.description: The first six months of 2026 saw a steep rise in the number of civilians killed or wounded in the war between Russia and Ukraine, the UN said Tuesday. Both Moscow and Kyiv have increased the number of air strikes targeting vital infrastructure as the Russian invasion grinds into its fifth year.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia-Ukraine war: First half of 2026 sees sharp rise in civilian casualties

events.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby’s mother was not supported to protect her children while escaping domestic violence, review finds
events.description: Interim report not able to draw ‘nexus’ between matters referred to child protection and circumstances leading to five-year-old’s alleged murder •Warning: This story contains references to Indigenous Australians who have died Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Kumanjayi Little Baby’s mother was “effectively” left alone to protect her children while also experiencing domestic violence, because child protection authorities failed to adequately assess multiple threats to her safety, a draft review has found. But the interim report, tabled in parliament on Tuesday, found that despite those conclusions, the review was unable to draw a link between the child protection referrals and the five-year-old Warlpiri girl’s death. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Kumanjayi Little Baby’s mother was not supported to protect her children while escaping domestic violence, review finds

events.title: Rescue crews in Afghanistan search for missing, dead in rubble left by flash flooding
events.description: Rescue crews in Afghanistan are searching for survivors and bodies among the rubble of buildings destroyed by devastating flash floods that authorities say have killed nearly two dozen people and left more than 100 people missing
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Rescue crews in Afghanistan search for missing, dead in rubble left by flash flooding

events.title: Flash floods kill at least 20 in Afghanistan and leave more than 100 missing
events.description: Flash floods in eastern Afghanistan have killed 20 people and injured about 80 more, according to Afghan authorities

events.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
events.description: Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’ Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show. The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget will affect individual countries for the next three years. Continue reading...

events.title: Tucson mass shooting has residents concerned over ongoing rampant violence, pols debating gun rights

events.ticker_label: 🚨 Tucson mass shooting has residents concerned over ongoing rampant violence, pols debating gun rights

events.title: ‘Front groups and fellow travelers’: state department attacks US left over Cuba
events.description: New report denounces Havana and claims ‘campaign of subversion’ has infiltrated highest levels of US government As part of the Trump administration’s continuing attack on left-leaning groups, the US state department has listed several activists and organizations as “front groups and fellow travelers” for Cuba, including Hasan Piker and Amazon Labor Union founder Chris Smalls. In a 100-page report released on Monday, the state department denounces Cuba as the “capital of 21st-century communism”, accusing the Cuban government of carrying out a “campaign of subversion” that has “infiltrated the highest reaches of the US government, recruited and cultivated generations of American activists [and] backed an unprecedented wave of leftwing terrorism on American soil”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 ‘Front groups and fellow travelers’: state department attacks US left over Cuba

events.title: Ebola outbreak fastest growing epidemic on record as deaths rise again
events.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has now claimed 930 lives, the country's health ministry has said.

events.title: Conflicts and energy shocks threaten progress against hunger, UN agency head warns
events.description: Conflicts and disruptions to global energy markets could push millions more people into hunger by driving up fuel, fertilizer and food costs

events.title: India's 'cockroach' protest continues a day after police crackdown

events.title: ‘I consider it a miracle’: Pregnant woman pulled from post-quake rubble in Venezuela

events.title: Why the youth jobs crisis is becoming global
events.description: Young people in much of the world face growing competition for entry-level jobs as graduate numbers rise, hiring slows and AI-driven disruption adds uncertainty to career prospects.

events.title: 'We're living in fear': 19 million children in Nigeria are out of school to avoid kidnappings
events.description: The 46 children and teachers that were abducted on May 15th in southwestern state of Oyo were rescued and reunited with their families on July 10th. Since the dramatic abduction of the Chibok schoolgirls in 2014, Nigeria has been hit with waves of mass kidnappings from schools. 27% of children in Nigeria are out of school, one of the highest rates in the world. The threat of abduction serves as one of the biggest obstacles to children pursuing their education.

events.title: US-Iran war: UN chief calls for new diplomatic push following attacks on civilian areas
events.description: The UN Secretary-General remains deeply concerned by the continuing deadly military escalation between Iran and the United States, his spokesperson said on Friday as attacks destroyed key civilian and military infrastructure across the Gulf region.

events.title: World News in Brief: Appeal for calm in Kashmir, women on the move, Ebola deaths mount, 12th anniversary of MH17

events.title: Protests erupt in Bologna after Moroccan man dies under police restraint
events.description: Human rights campaigners and Italy's opposition parties are demanding answers regarding the death of a man who died under police restraint as he pleaded for help. The incident was likened to George Floyd's murder in the United States in 2020, and to tactics used by the US Immigration police (ICE) under President Donald Trump.As anger has grown over his death, violence protests have sparked in Bologna. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Protests erupt in Bologna after Moroccan man dies under police restraint

events.title: Middle East war stuck in 'cycle of enhanced risk' as US and Iran exchange strikes
events.description: The US has bombed Iran for a tenth consecutive night, following the killing of American soldiers in Jordan and Iraq. Washington continues to call for an end to Iranian attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. For its part, Iran says it is at 'full scale war' with the US. Experts are concerned that there is no easy off-ramp, although both countries show signs that they are looking to de-escalate.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war stuck in 'cycle of enhanced risk' as US and Iran exchange strikes

events.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
events.description: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers with large balls of hail. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave

events.title: Shanghai science forum photos show China's AI, robotics advances in rivalry with US
events.description: China's leading technology companies showcased hundreds of cutting-edge products at the World AI Conference in Shanghai — from advanced robotics to artificial intelligence systems. The nation's annual flagship AI event, which was held from July 17 to 20, took place against the backdrop of a heated U.S.-China tech rivalry. More than 1,100 companies participated. Chinese companies have developed more than 400 humanoid robot products, more than half the world’s total, state media said. Open-source AI models from China are increasingly challenging leading American ones as they are more widely adopted globally. ___ This is a photo gallery curated by AP photo editors.

events.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
events.description: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned. Continue reading...

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Who had the most goals and assists at the tournament?

events.title: Australia updates travel advice for Laos as charges announced over mass methanol poisoning
events.description: Distillery owner could face only three months in jail over alleged poisoning of Australian teenagers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has increased its travel alert level for Laos, advising travellers to “exercise a high degree of caution” due to the risk of methanol poisoning. It comes as a distillery owner in Laos was charged after a mass methanol poisoning incident killed six foreign nationals, including the Australian backpackers Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles. Continue reading...

events.title: Italy: Protests in Bologna over death of immigrant in police custody
events.description: Demonstrators are demanding answers over the death of 42-year-old Abderrahim Fakir. Local authorities have urged calm while the investigation is underway.

events.title: Israel and Lebanon trial 'safe zones' in southern Lebanon as part of deal
events.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun is in Washington to meet with US President Donald Trump in the first official visit by a Lebanese head of state since 2009. It comes at a moment of high stakes for the country. Israeli forces are now occupying a significant portion of southern Lebanon following rocket attacks by Hezbollah on northern Israel. Tel Aviv insists that the Iran-backed militia be disarmed before it withdraws, something the Lebanese army is likely unable to accomplish. As a result, Israel has launched a '"pilot programme'" for "safe zones", which effectively sees Israel hand control of three Lebanese villages to the Lebanese Army, as Beirut tries to assert its authority.

events.title: Ukraine plunges into political crisis as anger over defence minister's dismissal grows
events.description: Street protests were held for a fifth day on July 19 in Ukraine, over the firing of Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Fedorov was dismissed following frequent clashes with top Army general Oleksandr Syrsky, sparking indignation particularly among young Ukrainians, who credit the tech-savvy 35-year old Fedorov with innovative drone tactics that have given Ukraine new battlefield momentum against the Russian invasion. Fedorov has publicly blamed the 60-year-old Syrsky for his ouster. President Volodymyr Zelensky is now seeking to defuse the mounting political crisis and restore unity to the country's military.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine plunges into political crisis as anger over defence minister's dismissal grows

events.title: India's opposition leader calls for Modi to step down as youth-led 'Cockroach' protests press on
events.description: Rahul Gandhi, India's opposition leader, called for Prime Minister Narendra Modi to resign on Tuesday "for destroying the future" of the country's youth. The youth-led Cockroach movement continued their protests in New Delhi, refusing to leave their sit-in site a day after police used tear has and batons to disperse demonstrators marching towards parliament.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India's opposition leader calls for Modi to step down as youth-led 'Cockroach' protests press on

events.title: Morocco's Tangier hosts first edition of its photography festival
events.description: Tangier, Morocco's iconic port city and gateway between Europe and North Africa, is hosting the first edition of the International photography festival "Photo Tangier". This year's theme is "The Call of the Open Sea". The event will span two and a half months, from the 17th June to the 31st August, transforming Tangier into an open air creative space in which private galleries, museums, and public spaces come together as one big work of art.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Canada ready to ‘intensify’ trade talks as Trump reportedly prepares to hit more countries with new tariffs – US politics live
events.description: Mark Carney says Canada has made proposals to resolve dispute as tariffs are due to be imposed on wide range of goods Sign up for US Breaking News email Defense secretary Pete Hegseth , joint chiefs of staff chairman Dan Caine and agriculture secretary Brooke Rollins are also scheduled to speak today. They’ll testify in front of lawmakers on the Senate appropriations committee about Trump’s $88bn supplemental funding request, which would help pay for the war in Iran . This is the first time we've heard from Hegseth and Caine extensively since consecutive days of strikes restarted against Iran this month. Continue reading...

events.title: Spain's World Cup champions return home for parade in front of almost 2 million
events.description: The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.

events.title: Spain welcomes back its World Cup heroes
events.description: Spain has welcomed back its heroes. The day after their victory over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final, the new world champions returned to Madrid, trophy in hand. They can now also add a second coveted gold star above the team's national badge, to go with the first star for winning the 2010 FIFA World Cup title. For an entire generation, this was the first experience of winning a world title.

events.title: ‘Won’t back down’: Indian student protesters return after police crackdown
events.description: More than 100 students injured in police action against thousands of demonstrators during march on parliament.

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.title: Hanson’s support for Trump deportation policy ‘makes me sick’, Monique Ryan says – as it happened
events.description: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Alpine ski resorts in New South Wales and Victoria saw climate records shattered at the weekend with maximum temperatures up to 12C above average. Ski areas of Mount Hotham and Falls Creek set July records for their hottest daytime and night-time temperatures and Thredbo also set a new hottest July day, according to the Bureau of Meteorology. We want to see more Australian ideas commercialised, more Australian technology deployed globally, and more of the economic value harnessed here at home . Continue reading...

events.title: DRC president agrees to open national dialogue amid mounting political pressure
events.description: In the Democratic Republic of Congo, President Felix Tshisekedi has agreed to open national talks with the political opposition. The move comes as the Tshisekedi administration experiences intensified political pressure over its proposal to amend the constitution. The agreement to engage in an open dialogue marks a significant political step in a country where civil society groups have been calling for a national dialogue for over a year.

events.title: Nicaragua’s Ortega moves to extend 20-year rule, says no elections anytime soon
events.description: Nicaragua's President Daniel Ortega has announced that the country won’t hold elections anytime soon, a statement that reflects his effort to extend his nearly 20-year rule

events.title: 'Anti-ICE' army veteran started fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials
events.description: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was armed with two axes, a machete, three knives and fireworks, says the FBI.

events.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
events.description: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

events.title: UN says 144 migrants and refugees reported dead or missing off the coast of Mauritania
events.description: The UN refugee agency said Tuesday that 144 people were reported dead or missing following "tragic maritime incidents" off the coast of Mauritania last week. Two children who were brought safely ashore from one of the sinking boats had lost all of their families during the journey from Gambia.

events.title: 2 Colombian nationals arrested in stolen Mercedes linked to violent California carjacking
events.description: Automated license plate readers alerted deputies to a stolen Mercedes linked to a violent carjacking, leading to a chase and two arrests in California.

events.title: Lamine Yamal celebrates World Cup win with little brother who charmed fans
events.description: The three-year-old brother of Yamal has been hailed one of Spain's World Cup breakout stars by fans

events.title: How a 64-team World Cup could transform football’s economics
events.description: Spain, Portugal and Morocco are the cohosts of the 2030 edition, but we await confirmation of 48 or 64 competitors.

events.title: Andy Burnham to cut taxes on electricity bills, announces new UK cabinet
events.description: Burnham is attempting to act quickly ​to deliver on promises to tackle the UK's cost-of-living crisis.

events.title: Huge fire at Ukraine shopping centre after Russian drone strike
events.description: A fire engulfed a shopping centre in Ukraine's Sumy after Russian drone strikes hit the building.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Huge fire at Ukraine shopping centre after Russian drone strike

events.title: Argentina deny World Cup villains’ role after Samuel Jackson-led slants
events.description: Hollywood actor Samuel L Jackson was among those who criticised Argentina at World Cup, with racism among allegations.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
events.description: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

events.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
events.description: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy products Donald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products. The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement. Continue reading...

events.title: Mexican drug lord 'El Mayo' sentenced to life without parole in US prison
events.description: Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, a co-founder of the Sinaloa cartel and one of the world's most notorious drug traffickers, was sentenced to life in a US prison.

events.title: Spain won the World Cup, but Donald Trump 'cast a shadow'
events.description: Though Donald Trump mostly stayed clear of stadiums, the World Cup may influence the US midterm elections. DW looks at the political implications of "the most successful sporting event maybe in the history of the world."

events.title: Fake images circulate of Spanish World Cup players draped in Palestinian flags
events.description: An image generated by artificial intelligence shows Spanish footballer Lamine Yamal celebrating his World Cup win while draped in a Palestinian flag. People have also been circulating an image of player Pedro Porro, who they falsely claim is wearing a Palestinian flag around his waist.

events.title: Bologna's protesters call for accountability after man dies during police arrest
events.description: Clashes broke out between security personnel and protesters in the Italian city of Bologna. Demonstrators out on the street, calling for accountability after a man died whilst being detained by police. The incident drawing comparisons with the murder of George Floyd in the United States. Some viewers may find some images disturbing.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bologna's protesters call for accountability after man dies during police arrest

events.title: A former Ferrari boss has a blunt message: Your commute is more dangerous than his old job
events.description: Jean Todt spent decades around the deadliest motorsport on Earth. He was a rally co‑driver, then ran Ferrari’s Formula One team, then became president of world motorsport’s governing body. In his early years, a driver dying at the track barely made news.

events.title: Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid
events.description: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid

events.title: FBI boss details how agency secured record-shattering FIFA World Cup and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
events.description: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

events.title: Row escalates in Ukraine over army chief's future
events.description: Protesters are demanding Oleksandr Syrskyi be removed and former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov reinstated by Friday.

events.title: Can AI systems make moral decisions in war?
events.description: As more militaries adopt AI technology into their kill chain, Al Jazeera asks if they are capable of moral judgement.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Can AI systems make moral decisions in war?

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
events.description: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater, during a typhoon. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake

events.title: The war with Iran is escalating. What next?
events.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Suzanne Maloney of the Brookings Institution about the U.S. war with Iran, the increase in hostilities and what it means for the wider region
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The war with Iran is escalating. What next?

events.title: 🔴Middle East live: Kuwait accuses Iran of again attacking critical power and desalination plants
events.description: No description available.

events.title: 'A lot of aspirations, a lot of priorities, but already criticism' for new UK government
events.description: The UK has a new prime minister and a new government that seem eager to get started on its promise to change the path the country is on. Ahead of a first cabinet meeting, a cut in the VAT on energy bills has been announced, as cost of living as come out as one of the government's main priorities. But the announcement is already being criticised. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more.

events.title: What to know about the West African economic summit seeking regional reform
events.description: The 69th ECOWAS summit was held on July 19th in Freetown, Sierra Leone.The outgoing Chair of the ECOWAS Conference of Heads of State and Government, Julius Maada Bio, set the tone by calling for “far-reaching changes” within the West African economic organisation.The meeting was also marked by the Senegalese Head of State taking up the rotating presidency of ECOWAS, as well as by strong criticism from Guinea, which condemned the sanctions imposed by the organisation in recent years.

events.title: Man stabs tourists near the Acropolis in Athens, Greece
events.description: A man has stabbed and wounded two tourists near the ancient Acropolis in Athens, Greece’s top tourist attraction

events.title: Health response brings lifesaving care closer to thousands in Yemen
events.description: Thousands of people in some of Yemen's most conflict-affected communities are receiving lifesaving health care closer to home, thanks to funding from the UN aid coordination office (OCHA) through the Yemen Humanitarian Fund.

events.title: Haiti: Signs of hope despite gang violence and political uncertainty
events.description: For millions of Haitians, daily life has become a struggle to stay safe, find enough food and simply move around their own neighbourhood.

events.title: Peacekeepers step up support as families return to south Lebanon
events.description: With violence in south Lebanon significantly decreased since mid-June, UN peacekeepers there have returned to core functions such as protecting humanitarian aid and restoring critical infrastructure as families continue to return home.

events.title: Family identifies young woman found fatally shot in home owned by Rep Ayanna Pressley's husband
events.description: Family members identified the young woman found fatally shot inside a Boston property owned by Rep. Ayanna Pressley's husband as police continue investigating.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Family identifies young woman found fatally shot in home owned by Rep Ayanna Pressley's husband

events.title: Europe's 'green ideology' climate agenda risks crushing its industry, environment minister warns

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Europe's 'green ideology' climate agenda risks crushing its industry, environment minister warns

events.title: US service member killed in Iraq as troops recover unidentified remains in Jordan: CENTCOM
events.description: A U.S. service member was killed in Iraq while handling explosives from a downed Iranian drone as troops searching in Jordan recovered unidentified remains.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US service member killed in Iraq as troops recover unidentified remains in Jordan: CENTCOM

events.title: Incoming UK PM Andy Burnham rejects Thatcher-era policies, signals leftward shift
events.description: Andy Burnham condemned Margaret Thatcher's legacy and promised public ownership of utilities, housing and transport in his first Labour leader speech.

events.title: Japan raises safety concern over Chinese destroyer's live-fire drills
events.description: Japanese officials have expressed concern after a Chinese navy destroyer conducted live-fire drills off one of Japan’s remote islands

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Nicaragua’s president says country will not hold any more elections
events.description: Daniel Ortega, the 80-year-old authoritarian leader, says move will ‘build a wall’ against the opposition Nicaragua’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has said the country will hold no further elections to prevent the opposition from coming to power. The 80-year-old authoritarian leader, who has been in office since 2007, also said he would work with the congress he controls to pass new laws that would “build a wall” against the opposition. Continue reading...

events.title: France could become first EU country to ban social media for under-15s
events.description: If the bill passes both the lower house and the senate, France is expected to become the first EU nation to ban social media for those under 15. Solange Mougin has more on the ban and how it wll work. Championed by Emmanuel Macron, it comes on the heels of similar bans in Australia and Indonesia and ahead of a ban on smartphones in high schools. But how will the ban work?

events.title: The UK gets a new government as its prime minister vows to be a 'circuit-breaker'
events.description: Andy Burnham is now the UK's seventh prime minister in ten years. He has promised to present a ten-year programme offering a new political and economic model for the country, away from the neoliberal polices adopted in the 1980s. Challenges include tackling high cost of living, and the rising popularity of the far right. He has also nominated his government.

events.title: Trump slaps more tariffs on Canada over 'discrimination'
events.description: The Trump administration has introduced 50% tariffs on more goods from Canada, with an official claiming the measures are in response to the country's "continued discrimination".

events.title: German railway to ban consuming alcohol at all its stations, with some exemptions
events.description: Germany’s national railway operator says it is banning alcohol consumption at all stations on the network in an effort to improve security and cleanliness for employees and passengers

events.title: Haiti: Training the next generation to fight corruption
events.description: How do you tackle corruption in a country grappling with insecurity, weak institutions and widespread impunity? In Haiti, the Government and the United Nations are betting on the country's youth.

events.title: Bodycam catches Sunny Hostin invoking her legal chops as cops cite Harvard-grad son for trespassing
events.description: Bodycam video captures Sunny Hostin telling MTA police she's a former federal prosecutor after her son Gabriel Hostin was stopped for trespassing.

events.title: Inside underground El Paso storm drain system Border Patrol says cartels exploit to smuggle illegal migrants

events.title: Nicaragua's Ortega says country will 'never again' hold elections, shutting out opposition
events.description: Nicaragua's President Daniel Ortega said the country will hold no more elections, eliminating any path for opposition to challenge his rule when his term ends.

events.title: Tate brothers to remain behind bars for now as UK seeks extradition on rape, trafficking charges
events.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate will remain behind bars for now after appearing in a Miami federal courtroom as they begin fighting Britain's extradition request on 59 charges, including rape and human trafficking.

events.title: Dozens hurt in Bologna protests after man dies while being restrained by police
events.description: Thousands of people took to the streets demanding justice for a Moroccan-born man who died on Sunday.

events.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
events.description: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters. Continue reading...

events.title: Man of his word: Pope Leo speeches declared human-authored by Australian AI detection tool
events.description: Maps of Hope has been certified by Proudly Human, a company led by former chief scientist, Dr Alan Finkel Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A collection of speeches and writings by Pope Leo XIV has been certified as human-authored less than two months after Leo declared artificial intelligence the greatest threat to humanity. Proudly Human, the Australian company spearheaded by Australia’s former chief scientist, Dr Alan Finkel, undertook a review of a collection of writings and speeches by the pope, in collaboration with the publisher, the Australian Catholic University and cardinals in the Vatican. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Man of his word: Pope Leo speeches declared human-authored by Australian AI detection tool

events.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
events.description: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

events.title: How India's youth-led 'Cockroach' movement challenges Modi
events.description: What started as a satirical protest against exam leaks in India has grown into a wider movement voicing young Indians' anger over jobs and education.

events.title: India: Modi addresses exam leaks which sparked CJP protest
events.description: India's prime minister has stressed that action was taken to hold those responsible for the leaks accountable. Opposition leader Gandhi has meanwhile called on Modi to step down. Follow DW.

events.title: England and Liverpool football legend Kevin Keegan dies aged 75
events.description: English former striker and manager Kevin Keegan has passed away at the age of 75 after a battle with cancer. He was a two-time European footballer of the year with Hamburg.

events.title: Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine

events.title: India’s youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown

events.title: US Virgin Islands' housing authority accused of corruption
events.description: The U.S. government says it has suspended funding for the U.S. Virgin Islands’ housing authority after an investigation found widespread corruption

events.title: 'Today' suspect caught on camera before hate crime arrest, rap sheet reveals attempted kidnapping conviction
events.description: Andrew Truelove's criminal record spans two decades, including attempted kidnapping and making threats, before his alleged hate crime involving Craig Melvin.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
events.description: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
events.description: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Continue reading...

events.title: Germany news: Merz hosts Azerbaijan President Aliyev
events.description: Talks between the German chancellor and Ilham Aliyev have focused on energy. Meanwhile, Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt will present a report on sexual offenses against children. DW has more.

events.title: Moon Day focuses growing concerns around space junk
events.description: Amid surging lunar exploration, the UN on Monday urged governments to ensure that humanity's return to the Moon does not come at the expense of planet Earth – or the long-term sustainability of space.

events.title: Leaked Iran report finds record public anger as regime focuses on holding power
events.description: Iran's confidential report prepared for President Masoud Pezeshkian reveals majority support regime change or deep reform amid surging public anger over food prices and corruption.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: UK's new PM Andy Burnham cuts household electricity tax
events.description: The UK's new leader moved to remove VAT from electricity bills from October 1. The move comes before Burnham chairs his first cabinet meeting later today.

events.title: UK cuts VAT on energy bills ahead of prime minister's first cabinet meeting
events.description: Britain's new Prime Minister Andy Burnham kickstarted his programme to ease the cost of living for millions of Britons by planning to remove tax on household electricity bills. The VAT cut is meant to reassure the markets ahead of his first cabinet meeting, with the new prime minister saying it will be funded by the scrapping of his predecessor's digital ID scheme. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi tells us more.

events.title: From the General Assembly to Harlem: Mandela’s call to action endures
events.description: Sixty-seven minutes. That is how long volunteers spent serving lunch to people experiencing food insecurity at Refettorio Harlem in New York City last week – one for every year Nelson Mandela gave to public service.

events.title: Woke language overhaul would strip the word 'inmate' from New York City's entire jail rulebook
events.description: The New York City Board of Correction is replacing "inmate" with "person in custody" across jail regulations as part of a person-first language effort.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: Australia spent more than $9m for each asylum seeker held on Nauru last year – so why can’t they afford to buy food?
events.description: Many of the more than 100 people awaiting processing on the tiny Pacific island are skipping meals to make ends meet, a new survey has found Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Most refugees and asylum seekers held in Nauru’s detention centres say they cannot afford to buy enough food or access clean drinking water, despite the Australian government spending the equivalent of more than $9m per person last financial year. The group of more than 100 people have been sent indefinitely to the tiny Pacific island while they wait for their asylum claims to be processed. Many of the cohort say they are skipping meals to make ends meet, according to a new survey by the Asylum Seeker Resource Centre (ASRC) advocacy group. Continue reading...

events.title: Holocaust survivors president says exclusion of Jews from public life has ‘disturbing parallels’ to Nazi Germany
events.description: George Foster tells royal commission Australia’s democratic institutions fundamentally different, ‘but I can’t ignore certain disturbing parallels in the social atmosphere’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A rise in antisemitism across Australia has “disturbing parallels” with the social climate of Nazi Germany, the president of the Australian Holocaust survivors association says. George Foster, the president of the Australian Association of Jewish Holocaust Survivors and Descendants, told the antisemitism royal commission that he did not believe Australia was “becoming another Germany in the 1930s”. Continue reading...

events.description: China and the Philippines traded accusations after a violent altercation between navy personnel in the disputed South China Sea. The incident heightened tensions ahead of a major diplomatic summit in Manila.

events.title: Tate brothers to fight extradition to UK, says lawyer
events.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate's lawyer said that the brothers are victims of a "political witch hunt." He has vowed to challenge their extradition to the UK.

events.title: US imposes new 50% tariff on many Canadian goods
events.description: The tariffs will cover products including wine and hockey sticks, but not energy or fish. The move is the latest trade escalation between the US and its northern neighbor under the Trump administration.

events.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
events.description: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

events.title: Guterres to visit Cyprus as UN seeks to revive peace talks
events.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

events.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
events.description: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?

events.title: US ties to Israel rattle rare earth deal in Malaysia
events.description: Malaysia's rare earth refinery in Gebeng is essential to a strategic deal between the Pentagon and Australia's Lynas mining corporation. Now, Malaysian activists are using it as leverage to support Palestinians in Gaza.

events.title: Hungary prosecutors raid data servers of Viktor Orban's Fidesz, party says
events.description: Hungary's former ruling Fidesz party said prosecutors "showed up unannounced" and raided its data servers on Tuesday. The claim comes as Prime Minister Peter Magyar presses ahead with anti-corruption reforms following his landslide election victory over Orban in April.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Hungary prosecutors raid data servers of Viktor Orban's Fidesz, party says

events.title: Parents urged to stop buying  e-scooters as 'toys' as Ireland brings in new age limit
events.description: Ireland's deputy prime minister has urged parents to stop buying electric scooters as "toys" for their children ahead of new age limits coming into force.

events.title: Why last two years are worrying for future of Arctic
events.description: Arctic winter sea ice is back in rapid decline after a slowdown in melting at the start of the decade, scientists have found.

events.title: 'One in 15 million chance' identical quadruplets born in Australia
events.description: An Australian woman has given birth to identical quadruplets conceived naturally from a single embryo - in a one in 15 million chance occurrence.

events.title: Turkish deposed opposition leader plans to create a new party to take on Erdogan
events.description: The deposed leader of Turkey's main opposition has announced plans to form a new political party to challenge President Recep Tayyip Erdogan

events.title: Descendant of Nazi opponent tries to remove far-right party's wreath at Hitler plot memorial event
events.description: A descendant of an executed opponent of the Nazis tried to remove a wreath from the far-right Alternative for Germany party at an event in Berlin marking the 82nd anniversary of the failed attempt to kill Nazi dictator Adolf Hitler
events.ticker_label: 💥 Descendant of Nazi opponent tries to remove far-right party's wreath at Hitler plot memorial event

events.title: UK leader Burnham gathers his Cabinet after sweeping away Starmer supporters
events.description: New UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham is gathering his Cabinet after removing allies of predecessor Keir Starmer from the government

events.title: Lebanon's president to meet with President Trump to push for Israeli troop withdrawal
events.description: Lebanon's president is set to meet with U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday to conclude a visit to Washington

events.title: Ski enthusiasts flock to sub-Saharan Africa and a snowy mountain resort in Lesotho
events.description: Lesotho attracts thousands each winter to its unique ski resort, the only such tourist attraction in sub-Saharan Africa

events.title: US pledges to crack down on Haitian gangs making money off social media
events.description: The U.S. envoy to the United Nations says the U.S. government plans to stop violent gangs in Haiti from profiting off social media

events.title: Baristas behind bars: Thai prisoners brew a path back to daily life
events.description: It looks like a regular coffee shop, albeit set up in an old red bus. Stairs lead up to a serving hatch, with a menu of drink options. A selection of jaunty coffee-related messages like “My one and only love: Americano” and “Miss you a latte,” adorn the side of the bus.

events.title: Guterres: AI must be shaped by 'all of humanity', not a handful of powers
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday urged governments and technology companies to work together to ensure artificial intelligence (AI) benefits all countries.

events.title: Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health
events.description: For Tanatswa Amanda Chikaura, mental health advocacy began with a personal loss: while studying psychology at the University of Zimbabwe, she learned that a fellow student had died by suicide.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health

events.title: Congress to put American Bar Association in hot seat over DEI rules tied to law school accreditation
events.description: Rep. Brandon Gill, R-Texas, called a hearing on Wednesday to investigate DEI's influence on law schools and the profession. The top witness is Michelle A. Behnke, the president of the American Bar Association.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Congress to put American Bar Association in hot seat over DEI rules tied to law school accreditation

events.title: Lindsey Graham's sister breaks from foreign policy playbook with a different agenda and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: 'America's Most Wanted' fugitive laid blueprint for vanishing before 20-year escape: affidavit
events.description: Ronald Fischer emailed his attorney, canceled a London flight and studied identity-change literature in the days before vanishing during his 2005 trial, according to an affidavit.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'America's Most Wanted' fugitive laid blueprint for vanishing before 20-year escape: affidavit

events.title: Repeat offender busted again less than 24 hours after release in brazen jewelry store theft case: police
events.description: Surveillance video captures a California teen allegedly stealing jewelry from a store less than 24 hours after his jail release on identical charges.

events.title: Jiu-jitsu instructor and pastor accused of horrific crimes against children in exclusive California enclave
events.description: Bail was set at $17.5 million for Emmanuel Marchica, a Hollister pastor accused of years of child sexual abuse across multiple community locations.

events.title: 'Long Island Lolita' Amy Fisher's son charged as cops probe possible microwave link in cat's killing
events.description: Brett Bellera, son of Amy Fisher, and his girlfriend face felony animal cruelty charges after their cat allegedly died from extreme heat exposure.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'Long Island Lolita' Amy Fisher's son charged as cops probe possible microwave link in cat's killing

events.title: More than 100 Haitian immigrants with violent offense and sex crime convictions deported: DHS
events.description: A July 16 deportation flight to Haiti carried over 100 illegal immigrants with convictions for child molestation, armed carjacking, and drug trafficking.
events.ticker_label: 💥 More than 100 Haitian immigrants with violent offense and sex crime convictions deported: DHS

events.title: Sinaloa Cartel boss 'El Mayo' sentenced to life, ordered to pay $15B in drug trafficking profits
events.description: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada Garcia received a life sentence and was ordered to forfeit $15 billion in drug trafficking profits.

events.title: Andy Burnham takes over as UK prime minister, calls for 'new political model'
events.description: Andy Burnham enters Downing Street vowing to shift power away from London, but his past criticism of Trump could create early friction with Washington.

events.title: Steve Forbes urges Trump to keep the Strait of Hormuz open at all costs
events.description: Publisher Steve Forbes calls for maximum pressure on Tehran regime, urges Trump administration to keep the Strait of Hormuz open as a non-negotiable imperative.

events.title: Trump administration touts more than $1B in UN budget cuts as reform push gains momentum
events.description: The Trump administration says it secured over $1 billion in U.N. budget cuts in six months, including a $550 million peacekeeping budget reduction.

events.title: Asian nation with 1,500-year-old imperial line insists only men can become emperor in policy revision
events.description: Japan's Parliament revised the Imperial House Law to cement male-only succession, blocking Princess Aiko from inheriting the throne despite public support.

events.title: Resource-rich nation praises US ties amid Washington-Beijing critical minerals race
events.description: Foreign Minister Thérèse Kayikwamba Wagner says the Democratic Republic of the Congo needs multiple partners to develop its cobalt, copper and lithium wealth amid growing U.S. interest.
